name: omd-connect-pipeline
purpose: Own durable connect-attempt admission, exact-candidate validation, and fenced publication without placing Git or checks inside the lease authority's long critical section.

fit_evidence: Connect has durable state, fencing, crash recovery, scheduling, and replaceable Git/check adapters.
The same safety protocol must serve CLI, MCP, and harness callers while policy remains replaceable.
alternatives_considered: Keep merge, validation, and publication under one repository token
Replace SQLite before shortening the publication critical section
Allow multiple writers to update one integration ref

boundary.owns: ConnectAttempt identity, lifecycle, candidate receipt binding, publish intent, and finalization authority
boundary.mechanism: Admit immutable attempts, request isolated candidate effects, accept exact-tree validation receipts, and publish with expected-old-SHA compare-and-swap
non_goals: Parallel mutation of one integration ref
Running Git or operator checks inside a deterministic reducer
Calling the scheduler globally optimal without an objective and oracle
Changing Task lifecycle semantics in the M0 evidence slice
boundary.state_authority: SQLite LeaseAuthority is the single writer for attempts and leases; IntegrationPublisher is the sole capability allowed to update a configured integration ref

state_model: The versioned ConnectAttempt FSM in spec/connect_pipeline_fsm.json is orthogonal to the existing Task FSM.

protocol.versioning: Attempt, receipt, event, and snapshot envelopes carry schema versions. Breaking identity or replay changes require a new major version and an explicit migration.

invariants: A published ref points to the exact candidate tree named by a green receipt.
A receipt is reusable only when base SHA, task tip SHA, candidate tree SHA, write-set digest, fence set, and check-policy digest all match.
Integration ref movement uses a sole publisher capability and expected-old-SHA CAS or an equivalent proved primitive.
PUBLISH_INTENT is durable before the external ref update is attempted.
A stale base never reuses a prior green receipt.
Cancellation and timeout do not abandon an unknown ref-update outcome; reconciliation runs first.
Exclusive HELD write-sets remain pairwise disjoint.

runtime.ordering: Commands are serialized per attempt; ref CAS attempts are serialized per (repo_id, integration_ref).
runtime.concurrency: Candidate preparation and validation may run concurrently across attempts; final publication remains a short single-ref CAS.
runtime.backpressure: A bounded durable queue rejects or defers admission with an explicit retry-after reason; no 10 ms authoritative busy polling.
runtime.cancellation: Cancellation is accepted before publish intent. During publish intent or reconciliation it is recorded as pending and honored after outcome reconciliation.
runtime.timeouts: Queue, preparation, validation, CAS, reconciliation, and finalization each have typed deadlines and terminal exhaustion states.

durability.persistence: Persist attempt identity and every accepted transition before exposing it as durable.
durability.atomic_commit: Commit dedup claim, attempt state/version, and outbox intent in one SQLite transaction where a transition requests an effect.
durability.recovery: If Git ref equals new SHA, repair forward; if it equals expected old SHA, retry from VALIDATED; otherwise enter NEEDS_REPAIR without destructive reset.
durability.effect_idempotency: Use attempt_id, effect kind, and attempt generation; same key with different parameters is a conflict.
durability.migration: M0 is observation-only. Later slices run legacy and prepared protocols behind a per-repository versioned rollout flag and retain old recovery fixtures.

failure_model: Invalid or stale authority is rejected without Git mutation.
Candidate or validation red leaves the integration ref unchanged.
CAS base mismatch produces STALE, not an implicit rebase or receipt reuse.
Unknown CAS outcome enters reconciliation and is never blindly retried.
Outbox failures remain observable after publication and do not falsify the published tree.

traces: attempt_id, task_id, repo_id, integration_ref, base_sha, task_tip_sha, candidate_tree_sha, fence set, transition, effect id, and correlation id
observability.redaction: Repository and task identities are bounded labels or trace fields; source content, credentials, and check output are not metric labels.

security: Lease, validation, and publication capabilities are explicit and separately scoped.
Validator workers cannot mutate the authoritative integration ref.
Caller-supplied shell strings are not executed; operator-fixed argv and policy digests bind validation.
verification: Validate the machine-readable engine, FSM, trace, and loop contracts.
Run real-code positive readback and injected-negative OOPTDD receipts.
Crash-test every cut point around PUBLISH_INTENT, ref CAS, and FINALIZE.
Compare M1 and later throughput against isolated-worktree GIT and lease-only baselines.
implementation_slices: M0: lock contracts, build reproducible probes, and record the current RED fairness and scaling baseline without changing runtime behavior.
M1: prevent older conflicting PENDING overtaking and replace connect busy polling with a durable queue.
M2: move Git effects out of coordinator critical sections.
M3: introduce exact-tree receipts and a short expected-old-SHA publish CAS behind a shadow/canary flag.
M4: add pairwise-disjoint batch connect, then a bounded adaptive speculative prefix train.
falsifiers: A landed tree differs from its green receipt tree.
A stale base publishes without candidate regeneration and revalidation.
A bounded workload can starve an older conflicting waiter.
Publish-token p95 still includes operator check time after M3.
The new path loses throughput or safety against preregistered baselines without an explicit rollback.
